---
title: Errors
description: Error types thrown by @justwant/cache.
icon: CircleAlert
---

## Error types

```ts
import { CacheError, CacheAdapterError } from "@justwant/cache";
```

| Class | When thrown |
|-------|-------------|
| `CacheError` | Base class for all cache errors |
| `CacheAdapterError` | The underlying adapter threw an error |

## CacheError

Base class for all `@justwant/cache` errors. Extends the native `Error` class.

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `"CacheError"` | Always `"CacheError"` |
| `message` | `string` | Human-readable error description |
| `code` | `string \| undefined` | Machine-readable error code (e.g. `"ADAPTER_ERROR"`) |
| `metadata` | `Record<string, unknown> \| undefined` | Optional structured data attached to the error |

## CacheAdapterError

Thrown when an adapter operation fails (e.g. Redis connection lost). Extends `CacheError` with `code: "ADAPTER_ERROR"`.

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `name` | `"CacheAdapterError"` | Always `"CacheAdapterError"` |
| `code` | `"ADAPTER_ERROR"` | Always `"ADAPTER_ERROR"` |
| `cause` | `unknown \| undefined` | The original error thrown by the adapter |

```ts
import { CacheAdapterError } from "@justwant/cache";

try {
  await cache.get("key");
} catch (err) {
  if (err instanceof CacheAdapterError) {
    console.error("Adapter failed:", err.cause);
  }
}
```

## Suppress errors

To avoid throwing on adapter failures, set `onError` at cache creation time:

```ts
const cache = createCache({
  adapter: redisAdapter({ redis: redisClient }),
  onError: "silent", // Return null instead of throwing
});
```

See [Basic Usage](/docs/cache/basic-usage) for all `onError` values.
